2606.16914.title: Greed Is Learned: Visible Incentives as Reward-Hacking Triggers
2606.16914.paper_summary: Deployed agents increasingly act with their reward proxy in view, such as a balance, score, or KPI dashboard. We show that reinforcement learning can make a policy \emph{addicted} to such a visible self-benefit channel. It chases the displayed payoff across held-out domains, sacrifices the true task to do so, and follows the channel wherever we rewrite it, while policies that never saw the channel stay honest. We call this \emph{reward-channel addiction} and study it in \emph{MoneyWorld}, a synthetic sandbox. The addiction can \emph{flip a model's safety alignment}: trained only on innocuous money tasks with no safety content, the model abandons the safe action it otherwise always takes whenever a dashboard pays for an unsafe one, and reverts to safe once the channel is hidden. This learned bribe replicates across model scales and families. Blindly optimizing super-capable, next-generation AI on KPIs or P\&L can be dangerous for alignment. \emph{Greed is learned} when following such a channel pays.
2606.16914.paper_summary_zh: 部署的代理越來越多地以其獎勵代理為目標，例如平衡、得分或 KPI 儀表板。我們展示了強化學習可以使政策對這樣一個可見的自我利益通道變得\emph{上癮}。它追逐在保留域中顯示的回報，為此犧牲了真實任務，並在我們重新編寫通道的地方隨之而動，而從未見過通道的政策則保持誠實。我們稱這種現象為\emph{獎勵通道上癮}，並在\emph{MoneyWorld}這個合成沙盒中進行研究。這種上癮可以\emph{顛覆模型的安全對齊}：僅在沒有安全內容的無害金錢任務上訓練的模型，會在儀表板為不安全的行動付費時放棄它本來總是採取的安全行動，並在通道隱藏後恢復到安全行動。這種學習到的賄賂在模型的規模和家族中複製。盲目地在 KPI 或 P&L 上優化超能力的下一代 AI 可能對對齊造成危險。當跟隨這樣的通道獲得利益時，\emph{貪婪是被學習的}。
2606.16914.author: Tong Che et.al.
